description:
GOAL: User-facing process primitives: spawn(fn) -> pid, self() -> pid, pid type.

## Decisions captured

- Pid is a struct (in line with the "structs as foundational primitive" decision from .11). Schema: { pid_id: u32 (RawBytes), generation: u32 (RawBytes) }. Generation distinguishes a reused pid id from a previous incarnation.
- Could alternatively be a tagged inline value (carved from the special-tag space) — TBD by performance. Default: struct for now, optimize to inline later if needed.
- spawn(fn) creates a new process, schedules it immediately, returns its pid synchronously. The new process starts at fn's entry frame.
- self() returns the current process's pid via scheduler-side TLS or current-process pointer.

## Deliverables

- Pid struct schema registered in .11.2 SchemaRegistry.
- Builtins: spawn/1, self/0. Wire to scheduler from .19.1.
- IR prims if necessary, or just builtins.
- spawn(fn): allocates initial frame for fn, calls Scheduler::spawn(frame), returns pid value.

## Tests

- spawn(fn () -> 42 end) returns a pid; the spawned process runs.
- self() inside a process returns its own pid.
- spawn from a spawn — pids are unique.

## Out of scope

- send / receive (.19.3)
- pid serialization for distribution (far future)
title: spawn / self / pid (pid as struct schema)
